recentTweets.text: Just finished hosting a voting rights discussion at @milescollege with Shavon Arline-Bradley of @NCNWHQ! We are commemorating the life and legacy of John Lewis by ensuring that our communities are prepared to exercise our right to vote! #GoodTrouble https://t.co/hUkima2gDb

Thank you to @NCNWHQ, our outstanding panelists, and everyone who joined us at Miles College today for an important conversation on the threats posed by gerrymandering and voter suppression.

As we honor Congressman John Lewis during #GoodTrouble Weekend, we recommit ourselves to https://t.co/VXjfljhiwb

Six years ago today, we lost my friend, mentor, and hero, Congressman John Lewis. While John may no longer be with us, his spirit lives on in all those who continue the fight for justice, equality, and the sacred right to vote. 

At a time when our democracy and our voting rights https://t.co/2p2m1wcNoK

Shoutout to our amazing district interns: Kylan, Ora, Alondra, Elle, Kinsley, and Kamryn! They have been working hard all summer in service to Alabama’s 7th Congressional District. I took them to lunch today to say THANK YOU! https://t.co/FBQCkvUd2P

Great to join the National Black Caucus of Local Elected Officials in Birmingham! I was proud to share updates on my work in Congress, including our recent win to make housing more affordable for working families through the passage of the 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act.

Let's https://t.co/D4lOXQtvz5
My statement on President Trump's address on elections. https://t.co/TijThLl2R0

We are sick of it!

Sick of skyrocketing drug prices. Sick of rising premiums. Sick of families worrying that one medical emergency could lead to financial ruin. And sick of Republicans putting tax breaks for the wealthy ahead of affordable health care.

Democrats have a plan to https://t.co/xyTNYRtBdz

I am saddened to learn of the sudden passing of Senator Lindsey Graham. I extend my heartfelt condolences to his family, loved ones, and the people of South Carolina. Senator Graham dedicated decades of service to his state and to our nation. May he Rest in Peace. 🙏🏾

Trump is blatantly attacking our election systems just months before the midterms. He is purging the leaders of the independent agency that helps states run our elections and deliberately making it harder for state and local election officials to do their jobs.

My team is in touch with local officials, and we are

Cheering on Birmingham’s own Chris Richards and all of Team USA as they compete in the World Cup tonight in Santa Clara!! ⚽️🇺🇸 https://t.co/vgSg9DKCGB

If you were born in the United States, you are an American citizen. Donald Trump tried to undermine that constitutional guarantee, but even his right-wing Supreme Court made clear that the 14th Amendment is not up for debate.
 I welcome this decision and will keep fighting to

House Republicans are at it again, trying to ram through their voter suppression bill and make it harder for Americans to vote. I spoke out against this scheme on the House Floor. https://t.co/5hDcNbuvuq
So grateful to have Kylan, Kamryn, and Kinsley in our Birmingham office this summer! Visit my website to apply for a fall internship before July 6! https://t.co/0k4w9BbMWn

It was a packed house for today's @NCNWHQ briefing on how gerrymandering and voter suppression threaten American democracy. I was honored to moderate a panel discussion with Maya Wiley, Todd Cox, and Rev. Shavon Arline-Bradley.

It was especially encouraging to see so many https://t.co/QvDJ9EaEms

The Supreme Court made the right decision today by blocking Trump's illegal attempt to fire Federal Reserve Governor Lisa Cook. I continue to stand with her as she fights this bogus case. 
 The Federal Reserve must be able to make decisions based on what's best for the American

Great news! The Supreme Court just rejected one of Trump's many attempts to suppress the vote. This is a big win for voting rights and the ability of voters to make their voices heard.

We will continue fighting back against Trump's voter suppression agenda!

As Birmingham Tech Week comes to a close, I had the honor of participating in a fireside chat with @RepHorsford, @RepBynum, and TechBirmingham’s @deongordon.

We must ensure that every community benefits from the rapid advancements in technology that are reshaping our economy and https://t.co/2ABnSnj2bF

My statement on the announced closure of the BFGoodrich plant in Tuscaloosa. https://t.co/3on4VcovEX
Donald Trump is more focused on making it harder for you to vote than making your life more affordable. https://t.co/jjbm1UmRb6

Please join me in congratulating the winner of this year’s Congressional Art Competition for #AL07, Ms. Eden Smith of Paul Bryant High School in Tuscaloosa!

I was thrilled to welcome Eden and her mom to the U.S. Capitol where her artwork will be displayed for one year. Eden, we https://t.co/pWgoavMfzo

Black women have always stood up to protect our communities. But today, our colleague @RepLaMonica is facing up to 17 years in prison simply for doing her job and demanding accountability from the Trump Administration.

As we honor the emancipation of the last remaining enslaved Americans, let us also recommit ourselves to protecting the rights that generations fought to secure, including the https://t.co/7RSMuzTi6m

Fun fact: I was once an answer on Jeopardy!

The clue? From what state does the Member of Congress hail who knew both Barack and Michelle Obama before they knew each other.

Needless to say, @RepRobinKelly and I had fun taking a trip down memory lane during today’s ceremony at https://t.co/C2BMZjHvG0

Enough is enough. Trump and his allies keep harassing Black women with baseless attacks and political intimidation. Their sham "investigation" into ActBlue's CEO is no different. I spoke out against this abuse of power and stood up for the voices they want to silence. https://t.co/OlsAd9Dc0b

Team Sewell was thrilled to join DA Danny Carr and the League of Gentlemen for their 3rd annual Juneteenth Prayer Breakfast!

This Juneteenth, let us rejoice, reflect and recommit to the work that lies ahead! https://t.co/99SqG9UyKg

It was a busy day for Team Sewell in Sumter County! Our staff enjoyed meeting with constituents at York City Hall, sharing critical information and assisting with federal programs and benefits.

We look forward to bringing our mobile office to a town near you! https://t.co/nzIwSZtqVj

More good news! The House Appropriations Committee has advanced an additional $6.9 million for community projects in Alabama's 7th District!

Whether it's helping a rural hospital keep its doors open, ensuring an ambulance is available when someone faces an emergency, equipping https://t.co/4TAPBH8g8D

🔗 More here: https://t.co/a8ESpU2xyD https://t.co/x3RgpQANQf

Great news! We secured $5 million for 10 local projects in the FY27 Transportation-HUD funding bill approved by the Appropriations Committee.

This funding will help strengthen our communities, improve quality of life, and create new opportunities for the people of our district. https://t.co/R4ICZyHwE2

Monica is an amazing young leader with a passion for improving our foster care system and uplifting former foster youth. I am so inspired by her story and https://t.co/OwqmVBFwPn

I just voted NO on Republicans' partisan budget bill. Alabama families need relief, not another blank check for ICE.

This bill does nothing to lower costs, nothing to strengthen health care, and nothing to improve the lives of the people I represent.

My full statement. 👇🏾 https://t.co/6stt7E8q7W

I believe Jeffery Lee's crimes were horrific, and he must be held accountable. I am also deeply troubled by Alabama's decision to proceed with his execution.

Mr. Lee was not sentenced to death by a jury. Moreover, serious legal questions persist surrounding Alabama's use of

62 years ago today, peaceful demonstrators in Tuscaloosa were met with with violence while demanding an end to segregation. Though often overlooked, the events of Bloody Tuesday remain a vital part of Alabama's—and America's—history.

May we never forget the brave men and women https://t.co/LsWFL3NN50

Grateful for ever…
